rationale: The "POV: rushed home to play Tetris" hook skews to a Gen Z/teen TikTok audience and explicitly name-drops a competitor block-stacking game, which clashes with Bounce Blast 3D's 25-55 calm-puzzle demo and its physics/chain-reaction loop (not stacking). Palette (purple/blue/yellow) is loosely adjacent to the game's deep-purple+amber UI, but the live-action bedroom POV format adds a human character the game doesn't have and buries the satisfying ball-bounce payoff that is the actual selling point. Only worth testing if reshot to swap the Tetris reference for a Bounce Blast gameplay reveal in seconds 2-3, otherwise skip — single-creative, single-advertiser cluster signals this isn't a proven pattern.
